title: Add a default config file
suggested_labels: Type: Enhancement; good first issue; documentation
summary: mimir-rock ships no default config file and its pebble service runs '/usr/bin/mimir' with no --config.file, so the rock is not usable standalone (outside the charm). The issue asks to add a default config at /etc/mimir/config.yml, point the service command at it, and (per a comment) add minimal README docs on running the rock standalone, matching the agreed approach for all HA rocks.
why_good_first_issue: Maintainer-filed and well-specified (exact target path /etc/mimir/config.yml). The change is additive: drop in a small default Mimir config, add a dump/organize part for it, and append --config.file to the service command, then a short README section. No build/Go knowledge needed; validates with goss /config and 'just test'. Good newcomer PR with clear done-state.
repro_steps: gh issue view 61 -R canonical/mimir-rock; gh api repos/canonical/mimir-rock/contents/3.1.0 --jq '.[].name'  (only rockcraft.yaml + spread, no config file); gh api repos/canonical/mimir-rock/contents/3.1.0/rockcraft.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d | grep -nE 'command:|config'  (service command is '/usr/bin/mimir' with no --config.file, and there is no default-config/dump part).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: 3.1.0/ contains only rockcraft.yaml and spread (no config file); 3.1.0/rockcraft.yaml service command is 'command: /usr/bin/mimir' with no --config.file, and parts has no dump/default-config part. So the rock ships no default config and cannot run standalone, exactly as issue #61 states (target path /etc/mimir/config.yml).
fix_hint: Add 3.1.0/config.yml (a minimal Mimir config, e.g. based on upstream's docs/configurations or the demo single-process config); add a default-config part (plugin: dump) with organize 'config.yml: etc/mimir/config.yml'; change the service command to '/usr/bin/mimir --config.file=/etc/mimir/config.yml'; add a short README 'run standalone' section. Decide per the issue whether to apply only to the newest dir or retrospectively. Validate with 'just test'.
notes: Real maintainer issue (no label, 1 comment from lucabello asking for README docs, unclaimed). Cross-references tempo-worker-k8s-operator#71 and the same HA-rock standardization as loki-rock#81. Open question: apply to all versions or only new ones. The other 11 open issues are auto-generated vulnerability-scan reports.

title: Bump Mimir rock to upstream mimir-3.1.1 (newest dir pins mimir-3.1.0)
summary: The newest version directory in the repo is 3.1.0/, whose rockcraft.yaml builds Mimir from source-tag mimir-3.1.0. Upstream grafana/mimir has since released mimir-3.1.1 (latest stable). A beginner can add a 3.1.1/ directory copying the 3.1.0 layout and bumping version to 3.1.1 and source-tag to mimir-3.1.1, following the repo's established per-version pattern.
why_good_first_issue: Mechanical change matching an existing pattern (19 version dirs). Copy the newest dir and change two strings (version and the mimir- prefixed source-tag); CI/kgoss validates. Clear definition of done. Note the unusual upstream tag format 'mimir-X.Y.Z' (not vX.Y.Z), which is the one thing to get right.
repro_steps: gh api repos/canonical/mimir-rock/contents --jq '.[]|select(.type=="dir")|.name'  (newest = 3.1.0); gh api repos/canonical/mimir-rock/contents/3.1.0/rockcraft.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d | grep -E 'version:|source-tag:'; gh api repos/grafana/mimir/releases/latest --jq .tag_name; gh api repos/grafana/mimir/git/refs/tags/mimir-3.1.1 --jq .ref.
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: 3.1.0/rockcraft.yaml pins version: "3.1.0" and source-tag: "mimir-3.1.0" (newest version dir in repo); upstream grafana/mimir releases/latest is mimir-3.1.1 and the tag refs/tags/mimir-3.1.1 exists, so the rock is one patch release behind the latest upstream.
fix_hint: Copy 3.1.0/ to 3.1.1/; set version: "3.1.1" and source-tag: "mimir-3.1.1" (keep the 'mimir-' tag prefix); keep base ubuntu@26.04 and license Apache-2.0. Validate with CI/kgoss. If issue #61 lands first, the new dir should also include the default config.
notes: An update.yaml-style automation typically raises these bumps, but the newest dir is still 3.1.0 while mimir-3.1.1 is out. Coupled loosely to issue #61 (default config). Distinct, easily verifiable beginner change.
